sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check character position after RIGHT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

char_blue = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b'][0]
char_yellow = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'Y'][0]

print(f"After RIGHT:")
print(f"  Blue char boundary: {char_blue['boundary']}")
print(f"  Yellow char boundary: {char_yellow['boundary']}")

# The character seems to move in jumps. Let me understand the platform layout.
# From the ASCII earlier:
# Row 37: B2GB5GB4S6B2bB2S30...
# The character is between two sky blue regions (S6 and S30)
# The gap is black (B) between them

# Let me check the horizontal layout around the character
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nRow 37: {ascii_lines[37]}")
print(f"Row 38: {ascii_lines[38]}")
print(f"Row 39: {ascii_lines[39]}")
print(f"Row 40: {ascii_lines[40]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The character is back at the original position (blue at col 20-21, yellow at col 22). So LEFT and RIGHT toggle between two positions on the left side of the platform.
Looking at the layout:
- Row 37: ...SSSSSSBBbBBSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSSS...
- The character (b) is between two black (B) sections, with sky blue (S) on both sides
- LEFT moves the character to the left side of the gap (col ~15)
- RIGHT moves the character back to the right side of the gap (col ~21)
This seems like the character is jumping between "slots" or "positions" on the platform. Let me explore more - maybe I need to reach the green blocks at the top.
Let me check if there's a way to move UP or if ACTION7 does something special. But first, let me understand the structure better. The green blocks are at rows 1-4 and 13-16, while the character is at row 37-40. There must be a way to move vertically.
